title: Mandevilla longiflora
taxonomy: Mandevilla longiflora is a member of the Apocynaceae family. This family includes approximately 5,000 species of plants, commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. Mandevilla longiflora is a type of flowering vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is often confused with Mandevilla, a genus of tropical evergreen vines in the same plant family. Mandevilla longiflora is also known for its fragrant white flowers and is often used as an ornamental plant.
uses: Mandevilla longiflora, a plant species native to South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Its purported medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins. Studies have shown that Mandevilla longiflora extracts exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of the plant's medicinal uses.

side_effects: Mandevilla longiflora, also known as the rock trumpet, is a plant that has potential side effects when ingested due to the presence of toxic compounds. intro: Mandevilla longiflora, also known as the long-flowered mandevilla or Mandevilla, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in relieving pain and reducing the risk of chronic diseases. The bioactive constituents of Mandevilla longiflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Mandevilla is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
